name: LinearProgress
description: A full-width bar that spans a region — under a page header, a toolbar, or a DataGrid — to show that data is loading. Split out of the old repo's single umbrella "ProgressIndicator" component: the real library has two separate components, CircularProgress and LinearProgress, not one component with a type prop switching between them. karoo-ui's wrapper is a bare re-export of MUI's <LinearProgress> — no overrides.
keyRule: Use LinearProgress for a region-width indicator (under a toolbar, above a table). Use determinate when you know the percentage; indeterminate (the default) when you don't; buffer/query are rare secondary variants for two-stage or unknown-direction loads.

variants.name: variant="indeterminate" (default)
variants.whenToUse: Loading where duration is unknown; spans the full width of its container.

variants.name: variant="determinate"
variants.whenToUse: A known-percentage task with a layout to sit above/below (uploads, exports) — pair with `value`.

variants.name: variant="buffer"
variants.whenToUse: Two-stage progress (e.g. downloaded vs. buffered) — pair with `value` + `valueBuffer`. Rare in fleet-web today.

variants.name: variant="query"
variants.whenToUse: An indeterminate animation that runs in the reverse direction, conventionally used while querying before a real percentage is known.

anatomy.name: Active indicator
anatomy.description: the filled/animated bar segment showing progress.

behaviors.name: Determinate vs indeterminate
behaviors.description: Switch to variant="determinate" as soon as a real percentage is available via `value`; don't fake progress. Stays indeterminate (the default) otherwise.

behaviors.name: Delay
behaviors.description: For fast operations, delay showing the bar briefly to avoid a flicker — this is caller-side logic, not a LinearProgress prop.

behaviors.name: DataGrid loading state
behaviors.description: DataGrid's own `loading` prop renders a spinner overlay by default, not a LinearProgress bar under the toolbar — if the old MD2 'linear bar under the toolbar' pattern is wanted, compose LinearProgress explicitly above the DataGrid rather than relying on `loading` to produce it.

states.treatment: Animated sliding bar across the full track width.
Bar fill width reflects `value`; no sliding animation.
Caller hides the component, or briefly shows value=100 before dismissing — not a built-in state.

specs.value: primary (semantic.color.brand.primary.main, #F47735) — color="primary" is MUI's own default.
Always 100% of its container — LinearProgress has no `size`/`maxWidth` prop; constrain it by wrapping in a sized container.

props.name: variant
props.description: determinate/buffer need `value` (and valueBuffer for buffer); indeterminate/query animate with no known percentage.

props.name: valueBuffer
props.description: 0-100. The secondary (buffer) value, only used by variant="buffer".

props.name: color
props.description: Theme colour applied to the track/bar.

doThis: Use determinate progress when you know the percentage.
Prefer skeletons for content loads into a known layout.
Delay showing the bar on fast (sub-second) operations to avoid flicker.
Give it an accessible label via aria-label on a wrapping element — LinearProgress itself renders no text.
dontDoThis: Don't reach for a `type` prop to pick circular vs linear — they're two separate components now; import CircularProgress directly for the compact/inline shape.
Don't expect a `maxWidth` prop — size it via a wrapping container's width/max-width instead.
accessibility: Renders role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow set automatically for determinate/buffer; indeterminate/query omit aria-valuenow per ARIA spec.
Give it an accessible label (aria-label or aria-labelledby on a wrapping element) describing what's loading.
Don't rely on motion alone; set aria-busy on the region that's loading.

related.name: CircularProgress
related.why: The compact/inline sibling — use for a small spinner instead of a region-width bar

related.name: DataGrid
related.why: Compose LinearProgress above it for the classic 'bar under the toolbar' loading treatment; DataGrid's own `loading` prop renders a different overlay by default

related.name: Snackbar
related.why: Announce completion when the wait ends
